title: Thunderbird profile on other HDD. How to access it after Win10 crash and new setup?

Thanks on beforehand.

title: Converting POP accounts to IMAP accounts
creator: jmallard
content:
I have 6 email accounts in Thunderbird (TB). 4 recently created IMAP accounts and two POP accounts.

I recently added an IMAP email client for my domain name. There already exists a POP account for this domain name and I’ve had it since TB came out (I used Pegasus Mail before).

The account names are displayed the same in TB except the IMAP account. "___@___.com" The above is the POP account and it only displays the email name: "___@___com"

"___@___.com IMAP" The above IMAP account also displays the word “IMAP” in the title.

I have over 46000 messages in 58 sub folders on this POP account and I want to keep all of them for now.

My biggest question is - when I Delete the POP account, "will TB also delete all the sub folders at the same time ?"

Right now, the Inbox on the IMAP account does not retain any messages. When I start TB, we know it auto checks all email accounts and downloads new mail to each account. Emails to my domain name go to the IMAP account first. When I open the Inbox in the IMAP account, I can see all the downloaded messages but only for a second. They disappear from IMAP and transfer to the POP account.

Every 10 minutes after that, TB downloads messages from all accounts again. This time any messages to the IMAP account can be opened & viewed. They can also be deleted or moved to another folder. 3 to 5 minutes later, they ALL appear in the POP account. Any messages not deleted remain in the POP account Inbox. No messages remain in the Inbox for the IMAP account.

I know I need to move all the folders and sub folders from the POP account display to the IMAP account display. This is going to take a while. Like I said, the Inbox will not retain any mail, it automatically moves to the POP account after 3 to 5 minutes.

I have moved the ‘Sent’ mail folder to the IMAP account and started on some of my Archives files. The first one I moved from POP to IMAP was a ‘Sent 2008-2015' folder. This folder has 5363 messages. I just did a ‘drag and drop’ with my left mouse button from the POP Archive folder to the IMAP Archive folder. It worked like a charm. Took a couple of minutes and you could see TB counting off the messages being moved at the bottom of the screen.

Seem like TB was teasing me, it will no longer copy large count sub folders with ‘drag and drop’. I think that first folder is the largest. TB will sometimes create the proper folder name but not move any messages. I have to go in and select 100 or so messages at a time and 'drag and drop' them into the newly created folder in the IMAP account. Sometime I ‘drag and drop’ and TB doesn’t do anything. It going to be tedious to get all my email moved from POP to IMAP. Of course, I will do it if necessary but is there some other method of moving the sub folders from the POP to the IMAP account that is faster ?

I created IMAP accounts for my two other POP accounts and I only had to move the Inbox mail from POP to IMAP. One of the POP accounts only had the Inbox and Trash folders and the other one had those 2 plus five sub folders. One of these folders have 3381 messages. Had to move them 100 or so at a time. The other 4 folders had a small number of messages and transferred easily. Deleting the POP accounts at that point was no problem.

So, again, my biggest concern is deleting the main POP account and TB deleting the sub folders. If I have to get them moved before deleting, so be it.

Thanks for any help.

title: How does Thunderbird resolve DNS queries when a SOCKS v5 proxy is setup in the Thunderbird settings?
creator: dryzler
content:
Hello,

i am a little bit confused.. I have installed Thunderbird on a machine that has absolutely NO "real" network connection except of a VirtualBox Host-Only-Network Adapter that is used by a virtual machine on which the SOCKS v5 proxy service is running.

In order to receive emails i have setup Thunderbird to use that SOCKS v5 proxy WITHOUT the option "When using SOCKS v5, use the proxy for DNS requests".

So in my opinion, the machine with no network at all but with Thunderbird running has to resolve the ip adress of the mail server with this proxy configuration, and because that machine has no network it should not be able to access the mail server..

But in fact DNS resolving inside Thunderbird DOES work with the setup i have described.

title: Angle brackets in save file name break saving on SMB attached storage
creator: PouchesInc
content:
So I have been switching over to Linux as Windows 10 discontinuation is coming up. The problem I just ran into today is how Thunderbird does file names on Linux compared to Windows. Windows has the sender's email in parentheses (example@example.com) and Linux has the sender's email in angle brackets <example@example.com>. The problem that has arisen, is that when trying to save an email to a location that is attached via smb (for example: smb://192.168.1.111/Network_Attached_Files) it fails. I can save the email locally, but then even trying to cut and paste to the smb location still fails. I found the problem to be the angle brackets in the file name, smb just doesnt accept it. Thunderbird on Windows uses parentheses so the file name isnt a problem.

Is there any way to change how Thunderbird saved the file name by default, to change the angle brackets back to parentheses like it does in Windows? (when you right click -> save as for example)

Or is there a way to simply remove the sender name and email from the file name by default? So just the subject name and date is there.


I find it strange that I am the first person who seems to have come across this problem. I suppose saving emails on a network attached storage location is far less common than locally on a PC, but still Id think there would be at least a hundred+ people who use Thunderbird and use network storage that this would have already affected and been a problem. Is the angle bracket thing in the file name something new maybe?

title: Small GIF in signature marked as attachment when sent via gmail
creator: Chupo_cro
content:
Hi all,

I've been using Eudora for almost 30 years but from time to time checked Thunderbird too because Eudora was discontinued in 2006. so I have to use the OAUTH proxy running on another computer in local network.

I have now installed Thunderbird 141.0 and configured IMAP accounts for one email account I have since Yahoo! Mail's first days in 1997. and the one I have since gmail's first days in 2004.

Everything seems to be working correctly but there is one thing I can't figure out why it is happening.

I have the same signature containing an 18x18 pixel .gif configured in both Eudora and Thunderbird and they both display correctly on the recipient's side and on gmail's webmail.

The problem is - every such an email sent by Thunderbird is at gmail's webmail recognized as "containing attachment" and is marked with a paperclip icon which is not the case when email is sent with Eudora.

The consequence is - it now looks on the gmail's webmail as every single email that I ever send via Thunderbird has an attachment which prevents noticing the messages that really do have an attachment.

I don't see any way of inserting code tags here on the forum to post the HTML code of the signatures sent by Eudora and by Thunderbird once the email arrives into gmail's Sent folder so I am sending the screenshot.

The upper HTML is how signature looks like in online gmail's Sent mailbox > Show Original when sent by Eudora and below there is how it looks like when sent by Thunderbird.

And here is the pastebin of the same HTML code: https://pastebin.com/4YHnNctC

HTML files attached as a signature to Eudora and Thunderbird: https://pastebin.com/17NuE1SY

I do NOT want to use an image from online URL with moz-do-not-send="true" tag. I want to use the .gif file from my hard disk sent in such a way it wouldn't be (by gmail's webmail) marked as an attachment and in a such way the image used for signature would be part of the message body - exactly what works well when using Eudora.

When the message is downloaded for offline reading the image used in the signature should NOT be "remote content", it should be part of the message.

title: Thunderbird constantly asks for password after enabling 2FA on web.de/gmx.de
creator: julianw000
content:
Hi everyone, since I enabled two-factor authentication (2FA) on my email accounts at web.de and gmx.de, I can no longer use these accounts reliably in Mozilla Thunderbird.

I set up the accounts using an application-specific password (a so-called "one-time password"), as provided by web.de/gmx.de when 2FA is active. The initial setup works, and I can send and receive emails for a short time. But shortly after, Thunderbird asks me for a new password again — and this keeps repeating.

I also tried using my regular account password, but of course that doesn’t work either, since Thunderbird has no way of entering the second authentication step required by 2FA.

Interestingly, everything works fine on my smartphone with the Bluemail app. There I can use the application-specific password permanently without any issues.

Does anyone have an idea what's going on? Could it be that Thunderbird doesn’t properly store or maintain sessions for app passwords? Or is there another workaround?

Thanks in advance!

Technical details (unchanged during setup): Incoming Mail: Server type: IMAP Server: imap.web.de Port: 993 Security: SSL/TLS Authentication method: Normal password

Outgoing Mail: SMTP server: smtp.web.de Port: 587 Security: STARTTLS Authentication method: Normal password

title: perchè non si riesce a configurare la casella di poste certificate PEC delle poste Italiane su THUNDERBIRD
creator: marcoandreoni1956
content:
Nonostante ho seguito tutte le istruzioni publicate sia delle poste Italiane ,non si riesce ha configurare la mail su THUNDERBERD ho visitato altri siti per vedere se funzionava, ma seza avere avuto nessu risultato valido . La domanda è come devo fare ?????

Dettagli della configurazione: 

   Apri Thunderbird: Avvia il programma di posta elettronica Mozilla Thunderbird.

Accedi alle Impostazioni Account: Vai su "Strumenti" e poi "Impostazioni account". Aggiungi un nuovo account: Clicca su "Azioni account" e seleziona "Aggiungi account di posta". Inserisci i tuoi dati: 

   Nome: Inserisci il tuo nome o il nome della tua azienda.

Indirizzo PEC: Inserisci il tuo indirizzo PEC completo (es. nomecasella@postacertificata.com). Password: Inserisci la password della tua PEC.

Configurazione manuale: Clicca su "Configura manualmente". Server in entrata: 

   Protocollo: Scegli IMAP (consigliato) o POP3.

Nome server: mail.postecert.it. Porta: 993 (IMAP) o 995 (POP3). SSL: Abilita SSL/TLS. Autenticazione: Password normale. Nome utente: Il tuo indirizzo PEC completo.

Server in uscita: 

   Nome server: mail.postecert.it.

Porta: 465. SSL: Abilita SSL/TLS. Autenticazione: Password normale. Nome utente: Il tuo indirizzo PEC completo.

Completa la configurazione: Clicca su "Fatto" o "Riesamina" e poi "Fatto".

Note importanti: 

   Verifica sempre che il server in uscita (SMTP) sia configurato correttamente con autenticazione, altrimenti potresti non riuscire ad inviare email.

Assicurati di aver attivato la password per l'app se stai usando un'autenticazione a due fattori, come spiegato da Aruba. Se hai problemi, controlla le impostazioni del tuo provider PEC per confermare i parametri corretti, come indicato da Guide pec.it. Riavvia Thunderbird dopo la configurazione per assicurarti che le modifiche abbiano effetto, come raccomandato da Poste-Certificate.it. 

   PEC privati - Poste Italiane - Postecert certificati    Puoi accedere alla casella PEC sia attraverso un'interfaccia web sia attraverso il tuo client di posta elettronica (Outlook, Thun...

Poste Italiane GUIDA alla Configurazione casella PEC su Thunderbird Dopo aver avviato Thunderbird cliccare sulla voce Strumenti - Impostazioni account – Azioni account – Aggiungi account di posta. N... PEC | CONFIGURAZIONE Postacertificata.com Configurare la posta certificata (PEC) su Thunderbird Poste-Certificate.it 

   Mostra tutti

Le risposte dell'AI potrebbero contenere errori. Scopri di più

title: Unable to sign in to Thunderbird email
creator: cm.edwards1
content:
Thunderbird has suddenly started recently to ask me for a password. And to my knowledge I am not able to provide this password as if I ever set up one some 15 years ago. I can never ever remember being asked for it before. Before it was taken over by Virginmedia.com, it was Ntlworld.com. And my email was and still is: [email removed]@ntlworld.com.

I now get this enter your password for [email removed]@ntlworld.com on imap.virginmedia.com. When I enter all the passwords that I have used over the last few years, I get a message that either my user name ie [email removed]@ntlworld username is wrong. Or the password is wrong. As a result whilst I can see on my computer emails that go back to say 2010. They stop around May 2025. And of course I have had newer emails sent to me since May this year..

I also had about the 19th of this July to create a new method of signing in to Virginmedia.com as they had us create it as they had amalgamated to O2 the Telephone people. So now we sign in with a common password for VMO2. With regard to this new password, I tried it to sign in to my email. That did not work either.

I can only surmise that being an old duffer I must have been and pressed the wrong button somewhere, months ago. I now realize that I generally normally never signed out from Virginmedia.com so I had not been aware that my emails were not being updated to my computer until being forced to generate this new VMO2 password. At 81 years old, this is all way above my capacity. Perhaps you might be able to help in this matter.

Regards Cliff Edwards. P.S. What is an Imap?

title: Feature request - Email filter/rules convenience funtion
creator: Daniel.Mack
content:
Hi, I used Thunderbird at uni time and have to use MS office at work. For this reason I was a long time with MS at home as well. But now this needs to change. Not keen on MS Copilot BS any more. I think Thunderbird ticks all my email needs. some things are different, some better some worse, that´s ok. Personal I think there is a small but very important convenience feature missing, and that's about rules/filters.

Currently if you want to set up a rule/filter you need to remember or copy all details you want to reacted on before starting to create the rule, the you need to 1) click on the email-homepage, 2) filter 3) new 4) type in a name 5) type an email/ subject 6) What to do if true 7) save

it works and it does what is should. However what I love in MS office is that this can be done in 3 clicks. The function accessible strait on the top and already pre-populated if you go this direct way.

What could be added to Thunderbird: if with a right click in an email, and "add filter" option would appear (for example under Organisation) and this brings you strait to the "new filter" window with 3 fields filled out: 1) name of the filter contains: the subject of the email 2)subject - contains: subject of the email 3)from - contains: sender of the email

Then you only need to change/delete a condition and 90% of the requirements are met.

It may sounds very silly, but I have to say this functionality in MS saved me so much time, with all "update" or "notice" email just been sorted away, maybe deleted or whatever.

Maybe I am rules/filter junkie but it definitely help with all the noise emails can create.

I hope i used the right forum, and maybe it helps to make a Thunderbird a little bit better.

Daniel

title: Odd new intrusive spell-check pop-up suggestion list in composer window
creator: gessel
content:
When composing a message I am getting a pop-spell check window that auto-fills the top (somewhat bizarre) suggestion. This is suboptimal for me, though it might be entertaining to compose auto-suggest random word salad messages from time to time. The heretofore behavior was to red-underline unrecognized words and permit a right-click pop-up selection for correction.

I run an ever longer list of plugins to maintain the core functionality I rely on for messaging and I realize this pop-up could be an artifact of an "upgrade" thought useful by a plugin, but before I go through a day of sequential plugin testing, if someone here knows how to revert this behavior, I'd very much appreciate it:

The insanely disruptive new behavior is the popup offering to correct "things" as "Things" [sic capitalized for some random reason, though I'm quite smitten with "Thï¿½ringens" as a "correction" to "things"]

The good (olde fashionede) spellcheck offers to correct the intentionally misspelled "thinglys" to "thingy" and correctly ignores "things".

Thing (ha) is the not-for-me version and the good-by-me version are both 128.13.0esr (64-bit), both have "enable spellcheck as you type" enabled, both are set to English (United States) (though, oddly, the order of the dictionaries is different), and both run roughly the same set of plugins, both are on Linux, bad-for-me on Ubuntu and good-by-me on Mint.

I checked Advanced Preferences for anything that might be inducing this odd popup and couldn't find anything obvious under "spellcheck" or "suggest."

title: Move to "Inbox" Again - Logic is unclear (bug1982162)
creator: schro.cat
content:
When right-clicking on an email (RMB), I see the option "Move to Inbox Again." However, the logic behind this option is unclear.

I've noticed that some emails initially arrive in my unified inbox (due to multiple accounts being set up), but after some time—perhaps up to a day—they are moved to the Junk folder. This behavior started around six months ago. I've been using Thunderbird for a long time and regularly update it, so this issue is relatively recent and quite noticeable.

Previously, the "Move to Inbox Again" option was useful for retrieving legitimate emails that were wrongly marked as junk. I would open the Junk folder, highlight the non-junk emails, right-click, and select the option. Thunderbird would then move each email back to its respective account's inbox, as expected.

However, recently I've noticed that when multiple emails are selected, they are all moved to the inbox of the first accountlisted among the selected messages, rather than returning to their original inboxes. This is a clear deviation from the previous behavior and leads to confusion and misplacement of messages.

Additionally, this option appears even when an email is already in the inbox. In such cases, selecting "Move to Inbox Again" moves the email to the default account's inbox, which doesn’t make much sense. In my opinion, this option should either be disabled or removed when the email is already in an inbox folder.

title: CONSTANT locking up while composing emails  SOLVED accessibility.force_disabled value 1 (bug1972457)
creator: kharrisma
content:
This is an issue that cropped up with the last update to T-bird. It retrieves and displays emails as well as it always has, but now when composing an email reply, T-bird completely locks up, can't shut it down by any means other than using Task Manager and killing the process. Before killing it, though I expanded T-bird's processes, and it listed five instances, one of which read "Not responding." When this happens, the cooling fan in the laptop kicks into high gear, until shortly after I kill T-bird.

Needless to say, this is intensely annoying and inconvenient. I belong to an extremely busy recovery group, and email is my primary contact with them, so this makes things a royal pain. I've had to resort to hitting "Respond" to open a new email, then firing up the text editor to compose the reply, then cut/paste that in and send it. Workable, but really... it *was* working fine, and now it isn't, and I haven't changed a thing, Windows hasn't updated, no new apps or anything... it just stopped working.

I've tried a number of suggested fixes, such as removing all add-ons (removed, not disabled) and themes, creating a new account (which worked okay for about a week, then it too started locking up). About the only thing I haven't tried yet is uninstalling T-bird and re-installing it, which I'll do if I absolutely have to, but I'd rather not go through the hassle of rebuilding everything if there's any other way to fix this.

Is there any kind of information or file/s I can provide that someone may be able to make some sense of? I'd REALLY appreciate it. I've used T-bird since I had internet access (back when Alta Vista was still a thing), and I love it - - no other email app offers the breadth of features that T-bird does, even paid apps. I'd truly HATE to have to switch to something else over this!!

Non c'è quindi il pulsante "invia", né altre opzioni. Non c'è uno spazio nella parte superiuore della finestra di composizione sul quale col click destro appaiano opzioni di visualizzazione. Thunderbird aggiornato all'ultima versione. Provato con F10 e Alt+F10 e non cambia niente. impostazioni server posta in uscita corrette, impostazioni generali e di composizione normali. Tentata reinstallazione, stesso risultato.

Uso Thunderbird da più di dieci anni, e non mi è mai successa una cosa simile. Non ho possibilità di accedere al PC, se non in assistenza remota e con difficoltà, ma a settembre potrò mettere in pratica eventuali graditi suggerimenti. Grazie in anticipo dell'aiuto.

RC

title: Thunderbird setup hangs on "Checking Password" (accounthub)
creator: Strider22
content:
On a brand new Linux Mint Debian Edition install, I could not configure my existing email accounts because Thunderbird hung on checking password. Searching the internet found many others with the same problem but no answer.

It turns out the 2SV was turned on but as I was always accessing from verified computers, I was not being asked for a code sent to my phone. This new install was not trusted and so it needed to complete the 2SV.

The simple issue was that I needed to turn off 2SV in order to connect to my accounts.

      • Is there a return code from Gmail or Yahoo mail servers telling you that it is waiting on the 2SV so that you can inform the users. Alternatively, after a 3 minute wait you can assume something is wrong and provide the user with a help pop-up talking about 2SV or lack of internet connectivity.

Thank you

title: Cannot update display name for certain email addresses even with empty address book
creator: kijima91806
content:
I am experiencing an issue in Thunderbird where the display name in the message list does not update correctly for certain email addresses.

Environment

Thunderbird version: (e.g., 115.12.1 64bit)

OS: Windows 10 Pro 64bit (version ...)

Symptoms

I deleted the entire address book (abook.sqlite, history.sqlite, etc.), so it is completely empty.

However, in the Sent or Inbox folder, some addresses still show the old display name that I previously assigned.

When I right-click the address → "Add to Address Book", the new display name is not applied for these specific addresses.

Other addresses work fine under the same conditions.

Tried

Deleted cache files (global-messages-db.sqlite, .msf, panacea.dat)

Cleared Collected Addresses

Created a new profile and tested again

Restored profile from one week ago

Result

In all cases, some addresses still do not update the display name.

Question

Is this a known Thunderbird bug?

Besides clearing caches and history, is there any way to control the priority of display name shown in the message list?

title: Duplicate Email Folders with No Messages or Sub-Folders Suddenly
creator: cp.root
content:
Hello all!

I use Thunderbird as a local archive for my email. I have two accounts in the application: my home email via my ISP (POP) which I predominately use and my gmail (IMAP) which I don't generally use. There are years of messages in folders and sub-folder under the POP account, but nothing under the IMAP.

I’m up to date with Thunderbird at v140.1.0esr (aarch64) on my iMac with Sequoia 15.8.

On 07/22/2025 I went to download some messages via my ISP POP account and saw that a whole bunch of my email folders no longer had any subfolders and were empty of any messages. Some were also duplicated. Please see the attached image.

I haven’t changed any settings in years, and I do not have any add-ins. One of the nice things about Thunderbird has been its solidity and simplicity.

After every session, I would empty my trash and compact.

Suddenly my email seems to be gone along with sub-folders while top level folders are duplicated.

Wondering if anyone has any suggestions on how to troubleshoot this? Since I don't do anything fancy with the application and with the suddenness of this issue, I can't image I'm the only person having it? I’m quite concerned I may lose many years’ worth of messages.

Thanks much in advance! CPRoot

title: Account setup in new Thunderbird release
creator: WoodardLT
content:
Signed on to Thunderbird a couple of days ago to see a message about a new release. Seeing no way around it, I went for the update. Thunderbird now opens to a screen asking for an account setup. Name, email, and password--which I assume is the main email server (AOL in this case). I fill this in, click on test, and Thunderbird comes back with an available POP3 configuration it found, which is not the one I have been using for several years. I try it out, Thunderbird tests the password and finds something in the password or configuration that doesn't work. I try a manual configuration the the correct pop3 and smtp addresses and get the same result. I am at a complete loss at this time. This is all I get when I try to open Thunderbird--none of the usual email boxes, calendar, contact screens at all. I cancel the setup and get an option to continue and potentially not have access to some features, or exit. I click on exit and a new screen opens with file tabs for email, calendar, address book, and tasks. Looks nice, but there is no data brought forward on any of these tabs, and email looks like it has not been set up (no surprise--no inbox or preview box).

Any ideas on how to complete his setup and bring the data in, or on how to go back to the previous release and uninstall the new version?

content:
Hello,

I have been experiencing repeated errors with my POP3 gmail accounts in Thunderbird. My main account retrieves messages quickly (within a couple of seconds) but my other accounts take several minutes (20-30 min) even although there are only a couple of light messages to sync. content:
I run a B2B manufacturing business.. All based online, they send us their job and we get it made. I keep getting enquiries from @gmail or @yahoo and they all look the same and sound the same. title: taskbar icon does not show all new mail
creator: Vachon
content:
For as long as I can remember the taskbar icon has given me a number of all new messages. Even those that I have Thunderbird move to specific folders as they're downloaded. Recently, however, the only number showing on the taskbar icon are new messages in the inbox folder and not in the lower folders, such as /bills/state farm or /games/EA. The only thing I've changed was an update, which I had the system do automatically. I didn't change anything within that update. What changed in my Thunderbird that only shows the number of new messages in the inbox and not in lower folders as well?

title: Folder update is randomly very slow when downloading messages.  (win11)
creator: DoctorBashir
content:
My Thunderbird (version 115.12.2) is running on a Windows 11 system. My profile has multiple accounts configured as IMAP to multiple email services. I also have the Status Bar and Activity indicator enabled. Randomly, both indicators will show busy for an extended period of time. When I view this in the Activity Manager I see one or more folders hung for 10 or 20 minutes downloading one message out of several. This occurs in multiple accounts and servers.

I have researched this and attempted the fixes presented. I have cleared the cache files and compacted and rebuild email folders as suggested, but the slow update still occurs.

Then I decided to try and capture a ProMon.exe trace to see what is happening. What I found was Thunderbird.exe was spending all of the time while hung access the following file: C:\Users\MyUser\AppData\LocalLow\Intel\ ShaderCache\4a5eb0e65e559fc08e65ecb2fa9d650817ae895582a7a7ede162866d3e47a05a, like it was stuck in a loop.

When Thunderbird is operating normally, it is accessing this file but only for short period of times. Research says that it is ok to delete the files in this folder. I did that and it seemed to help for awhile. But it started happening again.

Does anyone have any idea what could be causing this? Is there a Windows thing to look further into? Any help would be greatly appreciated.

title: Can't send SMTP E-mails from my work - but works fine away from work
creator: brjohnson1
content:
Hi all, Roughly two weeks ago I lost the ability to send e-mails through Thunderbird for both my Hotmail account and my work account which is also Outlook based. All incoming messages are fine and my accounts sync non-outgoing messages just fine (in fact, my typical way of sending e-mails is to save to drafts and then pull up the draft in Outlook and send it).

I know that this has been a problem for multiple users over the years and I've tried all the typical solutions. I'm currently using: outlook.office365.com Port 587 Starttls Oauth2

Although I have tried the workarounds that I've found in the discussion boards including: - alternative ports -SSL/TLS instead - no authentication (this worked for a similar problem many years ago)

To me, the most glaring detail is that as soon as I'm off campus, it returns to working immediately which would suggest that the work network is doing the blocking. I've talked with our T&I multiple times and they confirm that nothing that they no of is blocking my SMTP which means its probably a setting they don't know about. They can also log into the e-mail settings and confirm that my SMTP is specifically not blocked (which makes sense because it works off campus).

For context, we use Crowdstrike as our endpoint protection.

We are a small place and my IT group is being pretty cool by throwing some time and resources at this. However, since none of them have any experience with Thunderbird, it would be great to give them a list of things to try.

Thanks!

title: Problem with the display of downloaded emails (bitdefender)
creator: njtinker
content: For the last two days my emails have been downloaded but are almost unreadable. There is no longer any text in the Subject box or the Correspondent box and the content displayed is almost unreadable. I have tried uninstalling and reinstalling Thunderbird, running two different System scans but nothing seems to have worked. The corrupted messages appear normally in Mailbird and Thunderbird on my phone. Has anyone any helpful suggestions regarding this problem? It's driving me crazy!

content:
With 140.1.1esr 32-bit (on a 64-bit laptop) I'm getting some emails from Earthlink POP3 account corrupted and showing only as plain text. The bad emails all share these 2 lines which do not appear in other good emails:

Received-SPF: pas .

I have another laptop with 140.1.0esr 64-bit and it does not have any problems with Earthlink POP3 emails.

When I installed 140.1.0esr 64-bit on the "bad" laptop, the problem goes away.

I was also getting corrupted emails from a Gmail POP account but could not see what might be missing or bad in the source.

Just an observation, this suddenly started happening on Aug 11.

Here's a post about maybe the same problem: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/answers/questions/5520490/suddenly-most-of-my-e-mails-contain-unintelligble

Recently, I tried to set up a new account with an email address like this: webmaster@mywebsite.com. My intent, when I get this right, is to use it to receive email generated by users of my website or sent to it by non-users who might discover the address.

I've checked my settings, also in a screenshot.

I deleted the new email account but I still get errors. I've slept more than twice; so I don't remember if I even made any changes to SMTP other than to specify the Google SMTP server for the account I set up.

What am I doing wrong? What do I need to do to fix this?

title: Missing emails
creator: turvey.au
content:
Thunderbird version: 140.1.1esr (aarch64) MacOS 15.6

When I do a search for an email that was in that folder, the email shows as a search result, but when I click on the search result, it opens a new tab and says 'no message found' - although a preview of the email is visible in the search results.

I have tried restoring a backup of the local folders from my Time Machine backup, and while restoring those files did wind back all the other local folders and emails, it did not restore any emails into the problematic folder, even going back to my oldest time machine backup on the 26th of May this year - when I know the emails were still accessible - didn't help.

It's clear that the emails are still there, in some form, since the search function can sort of find them, however there is an issue retrieving and viewing them.

I am hoping someone can help! Thank you in advance, Claire.

title: Unified Folders
creator: rwillia211
content:
Not sure if this is the correct subject heading.

Thunderbird 140.1.1esr for Windows 11 - no add-ons I have 4 email accounts (2 with Google). For some time I have been having trouble with Unified Folders. From the Folders pane on the left of the screen, - the Inbox and Deleted folders appear to be working as intended, if I expand either folder, it correctly displays the 4 accounts, and if I click on either folder, it correctly displays the messages for all 4 accounts - the Sent folder also appears to be working to an extent, if I expand the Sent folder, it displays the 4 accounts plus a duplicate of one of the Google accounts. If I click on Sent, it correctly displays the sent messages for all 4 accounts - the Drafts and Junk folders are both problematic - if I expand the Drafts folder, it displays only 1 of the 4 accounts plus a duplicate of the 1 account. If I click on Drafts, it correctly displays the draft messages for all 4 accounts - if I expand the Junk folder, it displays only 3 of the 4 accounts with 1 of the Google accounts missing, plus a duplicate of one of the non-Google accounts. If I click on Junk, it displays No Messages Found even though I know there are messages to be found My problem is the inconsistent way it treats the Sent, Drafts and Junk folders. I can't rely on them to drill down into individual accounts. If I need to do that I have to go online to webmail, which kind of defeats the object of using Thunderbird. If I disable Unified Folders and use All Folders everything is fine. Is there any way to get the unified folders to behave as one would expect? PS I've tried deleting smartmailboxes but this didn't help.

Non è servito cancellare il file 2025.msf e far ripartire Thunderbird, non è servito il roll-back di Dropbox a date via via più distanti e non è servito il restore dall'hd esterno. Il problema è probabilmente nato a febbraio, Thunderbird non lo segnala, ad ogni archiviazione di un nuovo messaggio il file contenitore si corrompeva sempre di più. Il file corrotto è finito in Dropbox ed anche sul backup esterno. Quindi, se questa procedura di backup evidentemente non serve, che cosa si deve fare per evitare la perdita dei dati? Devo manualmente cancellare tutti i *.msf ad ogni chiusura? Mi sembra assurdo. Come posso tutelarmi dalla corruzione del file se Thunderbird non segnala alcun errore? Ritengo che sia molto importante introdurre un sistema di auto sorveglianza di Thunderbird, che so... qualcosa come un doppio indice, seguendo come esempio il sistema delle tabelle di allocazione file nei dischi. Sebbene io sia quasi certo che i miei messaggi sia impossibile recuperarli, chiedo comunque: suggerimenti?

title: downloading from Gmail to Thunderbird
creator: franklinccoleman
content:
I have just upgraded my computer from Windows 10 to Windows 11. Due to hardware complications (e.g., new motherboard) I had this done at a shop. I took the computer in about noon on Wednesday, July 30. The upgrade to W-11 finally happened on Monday afternoon, August 11. During that period I received 83 emails to my Gmail account, which of course were not downloaded to Thunderbird, and also sent 13 -- all directly to/from the web Gmail site. Since the activation of W-11 I have received 19 emails and sent 6 -- still all directly from the web Gmail site. Due to additional hardware issues, my computer did not come home until this (Thursday, August 14) afternoon. After I got it hooked up, and running, I brought up Thunderbird. It has downloaded the 83 received + 13 sent, from 7/30 through 8/11, but not done anything with the 19 received + 6 sent emails since then. When I turned Thunderbird off and back on -- or the entire computer off and back on, it has repeated this behavior. Currently I have 332 (83*4) received messages sitting in my inbox, and 52 (13*4) sent messages sitting in my sent box.

What do I need to do, to stop it from repeating this 7/30-8/11 download; and more importantly /start/ downloading messages since then? I feel fairly confident that the timing of the switchover from W-10 to W-11 is not coincidental; and that there is something I need to do in Thunderbird under W-11, to correct this behavior.

All advice will be greatly appreciated.

Thank you, Franklin Coleman

title: I have an odd setup , with lost of folders and filters and want to switch one POP account to IMAP
creator: modemjunkie
content:
I have been using Thunderbird on my desktop since the end of Eudora, but moving forward from computer to computer has created some problems.

Looking at Thunderbird here is what I see.

LEN POP which is configured as a POP account with the standard folders: Drafts , Sent, Junk and Trash There is also a folder called Archives with subfolders for the years 2021-2025, There are also a couple of special folders Like News and Events. and a folder called xxx NUL with about 10,000 emails for the years 2022 and 2023.

Then here is GMAIL which is setup as a standard IMAP account. (or some reason copies of some sent messages from the POP account are saved here.


Then there is LOCAL FOLDERS which contains again the standard subfolders, and then more than 20 folders which are populatde by mail filters from the inbox. It seem most of the mail here comes from the inbox in the POP account. (I often see a notice like" 40 messages moved from inbox to inbox."

On my phone and iPad I have my accounts set up as IMAP.

HERE'S THE PROBLEM

From time to time the POP account starts downloading thousands and thousand of old emails. Today it was over 29,000 messages. I disabled downloading of messages and ran the duplicate remover on each folder, and got rid of as many as I could, of of course as soon as I clicked "get messages" the 29,000 mail dumps started again. (This does not happen on my phone or iPad).

It has been suggested that I should switch to IMAP on the desktop, but I would like to filter mail into the same local folders using the old filters, which I have developed over many years.

QUESTION So, Is there a relatively simple way to do this.

Thanks, Len

title: REMINDER PANEL - IMPROVE USABILITY
creator: miker206wa1
content:
Please reverse the REMINDER PANEL column order to Improve Usability:

NOW: "Event description" (_BIG_ whitespace) "Event Date Time"

SUGGEST: "Event Date Time" (small whitespace) "Event description"

WHY:

"Event Date Time" is fixed-length (short) "Event description" is variable-length (longer)

The suggested column order brings the "Event Date Time" closer to the "Event description" and INCREASES READABILITY.

Thank you, -Mike Ramey

title: Issues with Thunderbird IMAP/SMTP Setup Across Regions (Domain: orbitalpost.com)
creator: aygideon14
content:
Hello Thunderbird Support Team,

I’m the founder of Jobgap, and we use Thunderbird to manage dedicated alias email accounts for our users under our registered domain orbitalpost.com. These emails act as bridges for job applications, and it’s critical for our global team to access them reliably.

Here’s the issue we’re facing:

I (based in London, UK) can configure and use our Orbitalpost email accounts in Thunderbird without any issues. Auto-detection works and manual configuration (IMAP/SMTP) also connects fine.

However, my staff based in Nigeria and India consistently encounter errors when attempting to configure the exact same accounts in Thunderbird. The most common error is: “Thunderbird failed to find the settings for your email account.”

They’ve tried both auto-detection and manual setup, but Thunderbird either fails to detect settings or won’t connect.

Technical details:

Domain: orbitalpost.com

IMAP server: imap.orbitalpost.com, Port 993, SSL/TLS

SMTP server: smtp.orbitalpost.com, Port 465 (SSL/TLS) or 587 (STARTTLS)

Authentication: Normal password

Username: full email address

What we’ve observed:

Accounts work perfectly in London but not in Nigeria/India.

This makes me suspect either a Thunderbird autoconfig issue, DNS resolution issue across regions, or ISP-related SMTP port blocking.

Questions for Thunderbird Support:

Is there a way to create a Thunderbird autoconfig file (autoconfig.xml) for orbitalpost.com so all staff accounts auto-detect settings, regardless of location?

Could Thunderbird be failing due to DNS lookups that resolve differently in different regions?

What’s the recommended best practice for ensuring Thunderbird works reliably for distributed teams using custom domains like ours?

I’d appreciate any guidance or documentation you can provide — we need to ensure Thunderbird works consistently across regions, as it’s core to our operations.

Thank you, Leke Ayodele Founder, Jobgap London, UK

What happens is that I'll start to write an e-mail message in Thunderbird, and at some random point (from a few seconds to a few minutes) it will stop adding what I'm typing, and soon the text that it was showing grays out. Thunderbird still works fine for all other functions (reading received messages, etc.). It only has the stopping responding problem randomly when I'm trying to write a message. I tried running Thunderbird in Safe mode, but the problem was the same...

Can you give me any help with a solution? This problem is maddening!

Vincent Andrunas

title: Intermittent SPAM sorting failures
creator: lotharkrepke
content:
Hello,

I run TB 141.0-64 with Windows 11 and am on the Release Update Channel. I've had Spam sorting problems for about a year, through many TB updates, and many Windows updates.... I run SFC, and DISM, regularly.

The Spam difficulties are of two types: Many messages appear in my Inbox, are marked as Spam, but are not moved to my Junk folder, so, I move these to their Junk home manually. Also, many messages appear in my Inbox that are obviously Spam, but are not marked as such, and, of course are not moved to my Junk folder. For each of these, I click on my Junk button, and these messages jump over to their Junk home to meet their new friends. Often, messages of this second category, are identical to messages that have been properly moved to my Junk folder earlier.

Finally - The the Spam sorting problems I've described, are intermittent. I can sometimes go for many hours with my Spam tools working as they should. Then, the Spam sorting system fails again, as I have described.

I express my thanks, in advance, for any assistance that you kind folks might be able to offer,

Lothar

title: Thunderbird and Maildir
creator: sjlebas
content:
Context: I have set Thunderbird (version 128.13.0esr) to user Mail Dir. I am using a RHEL10 based distro (Almalinux10). Everything is working fine with received email. I need to manually process the received mail folder with an automation utility I had written in the past that was based on a Maildir folder structure.

I read through the article: https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/maildir-thunderbird - and noticed that it was around 6 years old and could not find any updated articles re. the current status of Maildir and Thunderbird.

My question(s): I noticed that the Thunderbird Maildir structure under the email profile does not seem to have a folder named 'new', it only has 'cur' and 'tmp'. This does kind of make sense, as from what I understand, the 'new' folder is intended for messages that have been delivered, but not seen by any email applications. I am guessing Thunderbird has the email received in its user inbox, so it belongs in the 'cur' directory as it has been 'seen' by Thunderbird?

I was just wanting to confirm that it is that case that all email received by Thunderbird using a Maildir directory structure will always appear in the 'cur' folder. For reference, in my previous setup, fetchmail was retrieving email from a remote email server and as a result all email received appeared in the 'new' directory. My automation application processed all email in the the 'new' folder and archived them once processed.

Thank you.

title: Blocco dei "contenuti remoti"
creator: giuseppe.miotti
content:
Ciao e grazie anticipatamente per il supporto, sto usando Thunderbid 142.0 su W11 e credo ci sia un comportamento inatteso. Uso Thunderbird su una casella GMAIL con PGP: ho creato le mie chiavi private e pubbliche, che sono riconosciute da PROTON (servizio di mail crittografata che uso da web o client), infatti le mail verso PROTON sono correttamente decriptate. Ovviamente anche PROTON ha le chiavi PGP e scambia mail regolarmente con GMAIL, che Thunderbird decripta correttamente. Problema: se metto una foto nel messaggio email che mando da PROTON a GMAIL, Thunderbird quando la riceve mi dice che blocca i contenuti remoti e non mi permette di scaricarli (manca il bottone delle opzioni), ma me la ritrovo come allegato alla mail che posso aprire (vedi Thunderbird.png). Ho provato a mettere la eccezione dell'indirizzo email nelle impostazioni di Privacy e sicurezza dell'account GMAIL, ma segnala lo stesso il problema. Se mando da GMAIL a PROTON una mail con una immagine, in PROTON vedo un segnalibro di "oggetto mancante" (quindi non viene mandata) -- e non arriva NULLA, nemmeno come allegato (vedi PROTON.png). Come ultimo tentativi ho anche abilitato "permetti contenuti remoti dentro i messaggi" dalle impostazioni globali, ma Thunderbird continua a dirmi che "ha bloccato i contenuti remoti".

C'è qualche spiegazione? può essere che la crittografia PGP abbia problemi con le foto? (nota: Thunderbird mi dice che è tutto OK -2 lucchetti con spunta verde- nel bottone "OpenPGP").

Grazie mille anticipatamente del supporto!

Beppe

title: "Message Options" to view all headers.
creator: cwcjr
content:
The old Outlook app has a function of "Message Options" that allows you to view all the message headers, without opening the message, to determine if the Email is spam or contains malicious 'packages'. Spammers fake the basic FROM information, but within the headers you can see where the message actually originated from and which servers it passed through. It is a safety feature to prevent automatic 'confirmation of receipt' and other cyberstalking responses upon opening of the Email by deleting it before opening. title: Linux system install instructions need update
creator: jcoles0727
content:
Now that Thunderbird is delivered in .xz archives, not .bz2, the Linux system install instructions need updating. The extract command in Step 3 should be:

tar xJf thunderbird-*.tar.xz

note the upper-case J instead of lower-case which is used for .bz2

content:
Hello, After recently I installed ThunderBird after installing a clean installation of Windows 10.

I have managed to successfully connect ThunderBird to my Email Server, and its associated CalDav and CardDav server.

However when I try to sync the ThunderBird Address Book with the server, it only uploads information (new address book entries, edits, deletions). Changes made on the server do not get reflected in ThunderBird.

This includes the situation where the address booked entry is originally made in ThunderBird, uploaded to the Server, and then edited on the server (e.g. added extra character to the name field).

I can sync emails and calenders between ThunderBird and the Server without problem.

I have tried deleting the abook.sqlite files in my ThunderBird Profile, but this has not had any impact.

For avoidance of doubt, I am using the "Syncronise" menu option in ThunderBird rather waiting for the default automated 'every 30 minutes' setting.

Question: Can anyone advise how to resolve this one-way synconisation. I would like ThunderBird Address Book to update/reflect changes made on the server

content:
I have run into a calendar notification bug when Thunderbird is not executing. This started about a month ago and may be due to changes in the 140esr release (I was on 128esr previously).

Suppose I set an appointment for tomorrow at 9pm and ask the calendar to notify me a day in advance. If Thunderbird is executing at 9pm on day before, this works fine. If Thunderbird is not active, the notification is never made when I open Thunderbird after 9 pm on the day before the appointment.

This problem also occurs if I Snooze the notification and do not have Thunderbird running when the snooze period is completed. If does work if Thunderbird is open at the time of Snooze completion.

I have tested this on two different computers, a desktop and notebook, both running Windows 10. Currently running Thunderbird 140.2.0esr.

I have missed two appointments when Thunderbird calendar did not warn me about them.

title: Using Windsteam.net email on Mozilla via IMAP
creator: armor611suit
content:
For a couple of years on and off I have been trying to assist a dear friend of mine with his windstream.net account using the Thunderbird email client.

The problem is that while we set up everything quite easily, the account is added, all of his folders are populated and everything is perfect initially within a few hours to a few days....suddenly he begins to receive the following error message: login to server imap.windstream.net with username failed.

At first it will work for a while hitting "Retry". Then after a while it stops sending and receiving email. Eventually I am forced to remove the accounts and begin the cycle again. I can't for the life of me work it out.

So far: I have updated the program. Set up the account from scratch numerous times Updated and changed the windstream email password. Scrubbed the program and all related folders numerous times. Checked for any obvious interference from security apps (currently only windows defender running) Checked for any internet connectivity problems (there were none) Changed around the IMAP settings to use any other combination of settings that would work (there were none) Read all the Mozilla Thunderbird posts on this forum related to Windstream numerous times. Scoured the internet for any other help. So now I'm actually posting about it.

Once again, initially the settings below work just fine, there is nothing but green lights and check marks and everything seems to be fine.....until its not and the cycle repeats over and over. I would welcome any assistance from anyone with experience using windsteam email on mozilla thunderbird or a moderator or administrator if any review these forums. My friend has used the Thunderbird client for many years and does not wish to switch. I will include a few screenshots of the error message. Thank you in advance for any and all assistance! 

   Incoming Server (IMAP): imap.windstream.net    IMAP Port (SSL/TLS): 993    IMAP Port (non-secure): 143    Authentication: Required for both incoming and outgoing servers.    Username: Your full Windstream email address (e.g., your-email@windstream.net).    Password: Your Windstream email account password.

Outgoing Server (SMTP): 

   SMTP Server: smtp.windstream.net    SMTP Port (SSL/TLS): 465    SMTP Port (non-secure): 587

content:
After downloading Thunderbird for Android onto my mobile, I imported email accounts and settings (all, including passwords) from the desktop´s Thunderbird to the phone´s Thunderbird by scanning the desktop´s QR codes.

I got all the accounts and messages onto the mobile but not the primary password. On the desktop I still have to log into Thunderbird but not so on the mobile. There is also no way to set a primary password in Thunderbird for Android since it only displays General Settings.

Thunderbird for Android does receive emails so it seems to know the passwords to the servers.

I have the newest versions of Windows 10 and Android.

Any help on how to import or set the primary password in Thunderbird for Android would be greatly appreciated.

title: Can't write to IMAP folders on gmail
creator: ari.erev
content:
Hello,

This worked with no problems for many months.

Recently, probably only starting today, Thunderbird complains about inability to write to Drafts (or any other IMAP folders).

When writing a new message, after certain number of character entered to the message body, a "copying message to Drafts folder...." is presented at the bottom.

This message at the bottom of the screen does not go away... it is just stuck there (and the email message does not appear in the Drafts folder, on gmail). (as I said, it used to be fine, until today).

In some cases, also a pop-up is presented that says that Thunderbird can't write to the draft folder, and suggest to write to a local folder. (That's really nice, but not what I want...)

I tried, some solutions, such as making sure the folder name is "Subscibed" and doing a a right-click on the folder name and then Settings-->Repair Folder - but this does not help.

READING from the imap folder seems to work fine. I see new messages arriving at my gmail Inbox folder in Thunderbird.

Any idea? Is there a way to debug this to understand if this is a network error (????) or some wrong behaivor on the side of the gmail imap server?

Any guidance on how to provide more information in order to help the experts provide a solution/fix is most welcome...

Thanks! Ari

title: Thunderbird version 128 - Modificare Threading predefinito
creator: francesco.borazzo
content:
Thunderbird vers. 128 Windows 11 x64

Voglio modificare la modalità di Threading predefinita, sono andato in Strumenti/Impostazioni/Generale/Editor di configurazione

ho selezionato la preferenza in impostazioni avanzate: mailnews.default_view_flags cambiando 1 --> 0

non esiste pulsante Salva (come scritto nelle istruzioni) ho dato Invio e fatto clic su X di Impostazioni avanzate poi fatto clic su X di Impostazioni

Ho chiuso l'App Thunderbird e l'ho riaperta ma non è cambiato nulla nella visualizzazione dei Threading.

Ho commesso qualche errore? Cosa devo fare per eliminare il Threading?

Grazie, Francesco Borazzo

title: Google Login Nuisance Screen
creator: rhhutchins
content:
I am being plagued - AGAIN - by the google login screen from Hell!

How can I get rid of it? I searched online and found instructions to open my google account and change settings to disallow signing in to other apps via google. The instructions didn't match the options offered by the google webpage. I disabled the option to allow signing in with Android devices (I don't have any Android devices unless one of my IoT devices is Android.) I could find no other options to changes except some relating to Chrome, which I use only for web development testing.

I tried to login here as usual using 2 different gmail.com domain email accounts: neither worked. I presumed that the damned google screen from Hell was blocking them; so I set up this login that doesn't use a gmail.com domain.

I tried to send a test email from a gmail.com domain email address to other addresses using the gmail domain and to addresses that do not use the gmail domain. The test ran for several minutes without completing. The only mail I have received in TBird is to the non-gmail domain.

3 questions: 1) How do I get rid of the google login screen? 2) Is it blocking email? 3) How do I get TBird to work again with all my email addys?
